cardio.whyThisTarget: Exceeds Mont Blanc's sustained uphill pace requirements with multi-day pack weight
Builds altitude-specific leg endurance with pack weight for sustained uphill travel

strength.whyThisTarget: Multi-day pack carrying capacity over alpine terrain with comfort buffer
Knee stability and control for crampon walking on uneven glacier terrain

climbing_technical.graduationTarget: 3/5 confidence on exposed terrain with crampons
climbing_technical.whyThisTarget: Following requires comfortable movement on glacier and the Gouter ridge, not leading

flexibility.whyThisTarget: Hip and ankle mobility for crampon walking and recovery positions
Ankle mobility essential for proper crampon technique and injury prevention

cardio.explanation: Your 3-hour hikes with 2000ft gain show a solid aerobic base, but the graduation benchmark requires sustaining 2000 ft/hr with a 40lb pack for a full hour. Your current pace and pack weight put you at roughly 45% of that target.
cardio.keyFactor: Loaded uphill pace and sustained duration

strength.explanation: Your strength training 2x/week and moderate loaded leg capacity (rated 3) give you a foundation, but the graduation benchmarks require carrying 50lb for 450m and doing 20 controlled single-leg step-downs. You're at about 40% of requirements.

climbing_technical.explanation: As a follower, your target is reduced to 36. Your indoor gym climbing experience and basic scrambling comfort put you at 50% of the adjusted target. You'll need to build crampon confidence and glacier travel comfort.

flexibility.explanation: Your hip mobility rated 3 and ankle mobility rated 2 indicate average lower body flexibility. The graduation benchmarks require a 60-second deep squat hold and 4+ inch ankle dorsiflexion, putting you at roughly 35% of target.

cardio.startingIntensity: Start loaded hikes at 20lb pack, building to 40lb. Begin with 30-minute sustained uphills.
cardio.keyAdaptation: Build sustained uphill pace with progressively heavier pack — biggest gap is loaded endurance

strength.startingIntensity: Start step-ups at 25lb, loaded carries at 30lb. Bodyweight single-leg step-downs first.
strength.keyAdaptation: Focus on loaded carry endurance and single-leg stability for crampon terrain

climbing_technical.startingIntensity: Start with gym scramble practice and balance work. No lead-specific training needed.
climbing_technical.keyAdaptation: Build exposure comfort and basic crampon movement patterns — follow role, no lead skills

flexibility.startingIntensity: Daily 10-minute hip and ankle mobility routine. Start deep squat holds at 20 seconds.
flexibility.keyAdaptation: Prioritize ankle dorsiflexion for crampon technique — currently the weakest flexibility component
